page: Microsoft Azure E48s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ESv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor operating at 2.3 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB memory-to-vCPU ratio. Local storage consists of a bundled 786 GB drive, and the instance is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance tradeoff between single-core and multi-core execution. Single-core performance is limited, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance falling into the bottom 10% and Geekbench single-core scores categorized as weak. However, multi-core scaling is more robust, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression all achieving average ratings. Memory performance is a notable strength, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top 25%, supported by average memory bandwidth across both small and large block read/write tests.

Given the high memory density and strong memory benchmark results, this server is configured for memory-intensive workloads that do not require cutting-edge single-thread compute speeds. The qualitative cost efficiency of this older-generation Intel Xeon hardware lies in its high resource density, offering a large memory footprint and substantial local storage without the premium cost of newer CPU architectures. This makes it suitable for relational database operations, caching layers like Redis, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E48s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the ESv3 family, powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores running at 2.3 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per-core memory allocation, alongside 786 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Benchmark results indicate a distinct tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is poor, ranking in the bottom 10% to 25%, whereas multi-core and memory performance are more resilient. Specifically, the PassMark memory score ranks in the top 25%, and multi-core benchmarks consistently achieve average ratings. This server configuration is suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and caching systems that benefit from high memory capacity and moderate multi-threaded processing rather than high single-threaded compute speeds.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48s_v3 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 786 GB local storage. It offers strong memory performance alongside moderate multi-core processing capabilities.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48s_v3 is a memory-optimized VM with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 786 GB local storage, suited for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM density, dedicated Intel Xeon cores, and integrated local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor
384.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
786 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong memory performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
Suited for database operations, caching layers, and memory-heavy workloads
